---
title: "About GEMMA"
---

## Background

The GEMMA project (Greenhouse gas Measurement and Modelling Advancement) was
established to address a fundamental challenge in climate science: how do we
know whether reported greenhouse gas emissions are accurate?

Countries submit annual greenhouse gas inventories to international bodies,
but these are largely based on statistical activity data rather than direct
atmospheric measurements. GEMMA develops the science and tools needed to
independently verify these inventories using atmospheric observations and
advanced inverse modelling techniques.

## Objectives

The project has four principal objectives:

1. **Advance measurement capability** – develop and deploy next-generation
   instruments for precise, traceable greenhouse gas measurements across the UK
   and at key international sites.

2. **Improve atmospheric inversion methods** – refine the statistical and
   computational techniques used to infer surface emissions from atmospheric
   concentration data.

3. **Verify national inventories** – produce independent, observation-based
   estimates of UK greenhouse gas emissions and compare these with official
   reported figures.

4. **Inform policy** – translate scientific findings into accessible, actionable
   outputs for government, regulators, and international frameworks such as the
   Global Stocktake under the Paris Agreement.

## Gases and sectors covered

GEMMA focuses on the key greenhouse gases regulated under the Kyoto Protocol
and Paris Agreement:

| Gas | Main sources covered |
|---|---|
| Carbon dioxide (CO₂) | Fossil fuel combustion, land use |
| Methane (CH₄) | Agriculture, waste, oil and gas |
| Nitrous oxide (N₂O) | Agriculture, industry |
| Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) | Refrigeration, air conditioning |
| Perfluorocarbons (PFCs) | Semiconductor manufacture, aluminium |
| Sulphur hexafluoride (SF₆) | Electrical switchgear |

## Consortium

GEMMA is a collaboration between:

**National Physical Laboratory (NPL)**
Lead partner. NPL provides world-class measurement science expertise and
maintains primary gas standards for the UK.

**University of Bristol**
Atmospheric chemistry and inverse modelling. Bristol leads the development
of the AGAGE-based inversion framework and contributes to long-term
monitoring at Mace Head and Tacolneston.

**Met Office**
Numerical weather prediction and atmospheric transport modelling. The Met
Office provides NAME dispersion model simulations and integrates GEMMA
outputs with operational forecast systems.

*Additional partners and collaborators to be listed here.*

## Funding

GEMMA is funded by [UKRI](https://www.ukri.org) under grant reference
[XXXXXXX]. The project runs from [start date] to [end date].

## Contact

For general enquiries about the project, please contact:
[gemma@npl.co.uk](mailto:gemma@npl.co.uk)
